title: [RFE] Include pod name in `podman ps` `--format` and `--sort`
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I would like to use pod name not only id in formatted `podman ps` output and be able to sort that output based on pod name and id. The current alternative is to print pod id first and just pipe that output to `sort`. ;) Usable, but UX could be better, especially considering the fact that I have to match pod id with the pod name after in the second step.
```
#  podman ps -a --format "{{.Pod}}\t{{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}"|sort
40ba88f1b135  inf-gitlab-2201251034-app       registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/gitlab:latest     Up 2 days ago
40ba88f1b135  inf-gitlab-2201251034-infra     registry.lab.skarbek.name/pause:8.5-1                 Up 2 days ago
40ba88f1b135  inf-gitlab-2201251034-mesh      registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-dp:latest    Up 2 days ago
40dc4fdebf52  inf-minio-2201251034-app        registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/minio:latest      Up 2 days ago
40dc4fdebf52  inf-minio-2201251034-infra      registry.lab.skarbek.name/pause:8.5-1                 Up 2 days ago
40dc4fdebf52  inf-minio-2201251034-mesh       registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-dp:latest    Up 2 days ago
4abd13034fc2  inf-nginx-2201251034-app        registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/nginx:latest      Up 2 days ago
4abd13034fc2  inf-nginx-2201251034-infra      registry.lab.skarbek.name/pause:8.5-1                 Up 2 days ago
4abd13034fc2  inf-nginx-2201251034-mesh       registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-dp:latest    Up 2 days ago
4d598b8287dc  inf-kuma-2201251034-app         registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-cp:latest    Up 2 days ago
4d598b8287dc  inf-kuma-2201251034-infra       registry.lab.skarbek.name/pause:8.5-1                 Up 2 days ago
4d598b8287dc  inf-kuma-2201251034-mesh        registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-dp:latest    Up 2 days ago
b4ec41011436  inf-nexus-2201251034-app        registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/nexus:latest      Up 2 days ago
b4ec41011436  inf-nexus-2201251034-infra      registry.lab.skarbek.name/pause:8.5-1                 Up 2 days ago
b4ec41011436  inf-nexus-2201251034-mesh       registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-dp:latest    Up 2 days ago
fed1abdb00b5  inf-tinyproxy-2201251034-app    registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/tinyproxy:latest  Up 2 days ago

fed1abdb00b5  inf-tinyproxy-2201251034-mesh   registry.lab.skarbek.name/bootstrap/kuma-dp:latest    Up 2 days ago
```

comments.body: `podman ps` already has a `--pod` flag that shows pod ID in the output - is this acceptable?
To get the pod name you have to use `{{.PodName}}`

> Does `podman pod ps` help?

Nope, `podman pod ps` shows pods, not containers. I would like to get containers list with annotation to which pod each container belong. 

> `podman ps` already has a `--pod` flag that shows pod ID in the output - is this acceptable?

`-p`/`--pod` can't be combined with `--format` - it's ignored.

> To get the pod name you have to use `{{.PodName}}`

There is no mention of `{{.PodName}}` in `podman-ps` man page, only `{{.Pod}}` which prints pod id.
When trying ` podman ps -a --format "{{.PodName}}\t{{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}"` I got empty string in place of `.PodName` on both RHEL and Fedora current versions of podman.

Ahh, this is definitely a bug, then. I think we're not fetching the relevant fields from the DB unless `--pod` is specified, which means that `--format` and `--pod` being incompatible results in being unable to get pod identifying information via `--format`. Solution is probably to fetch those bits from the DB if `--format` is used, as well as `--pod`; there may be some small performance implications about this, though, it requires more DB operations for each container.
IIRC we changed that for the remote api so that pod is always set for remote. I think we could string search the format and only set pod when the format contains `PodName`,
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This seems to work correctly in current podman. Closing.
